title: Ixeris humifusa
intro: Ixeris humifusa, also known as Asian wormwood or creeping yellow thistle, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant in the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ixeris humifusa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ixeris humifusa is often prepared as a tea or used in decoctions to treat various ailments, and it may also be dried and used in capsule or tincture form.
taxonomy: Ixeris humifusa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Ixeris, which is a group of small, perennial herbs native to East Asia. The family Asteraceae, also known as the sunflower or daisy family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Ixeris humifusa is further classified as a member of the tribe Cichorieae, which includes other plants such as dandelions and hawk's beans. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants in the plant kingdom.
